content.full_text:
California State Senate

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 327-8315

SENATOR
CHARLES M. CALDERON
TWENTY-SIXTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT

May 21, 1990

Mr. Wayne Imberi
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

RE: STATEMENT OF ECONOMIC INTEREST

Dear Wayne:

     This is to confirm our conversation that the
undersigned staff of Senator Charles Calderon do not have to
file a Leaving Office Statement for the Assembly then an
Assuming Office Statement for the Senate if said staff had been
employed with the Assemblyman prior to his recent election to
the State Senate.

     Both Michael Burns and myself have current
statements on file with your office. Our current forms were
completed when we were Assembly staff personnel. We had
previously been advised by Senate Rules and your office that
we needed to complete a Leaving Office and Assuming Office
Statement when transferred to the Senate with our Member.
This also confirms that your supervisor, Carla, has been
advised and agrees with your position on this issue.

     Should you have any questions please do not
hesitate to contact either Michael Burns or myself.

Very truly yours,

[signature] MICHAEL BURNS          [signature] HAPPY CHASTAIN

sections.question_synthetic: Whether staff members who transfer from Assembly to Senate employment with the same elected official are required to file Leaving Office and Assuming Office Statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request/correspondence from legislator's office to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal question or conclusion sections present. Letter confirms prior verbal guidance regarding Statement of Economic Interest filing requirements.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether staff members who transfer from Assembly to Senate employment with the same elected official are required to file Leaving Office and Assuming Office Statements.

Both Michael Burns and myself have current statements on file with your office. Our current forms were completed when we were Assembly staff personnel. We had previously been advised by Senate Rules and your office that we needed to complete a Leaving Office and Assuming Office Statement when transferred to the Senate with our Member. This also confirms that your supervisor, Carla, has been advised and agrees with your position on this issue. Should you have any questions please do not hesitate to contact either Michael Burns or myself. Very truly yours, [signature] MICHAEL BURNS [signature] HAPPY CHASTAIN
embedding.summary: This is a letter from Senator Calderon's office to the FPPC confirming a prior conversation that Assembly staff transferring to Senate positions with the same member do not need to file separate Leaving and Assuming Office Statements if they already have current statements on file.
